Items.Title: ChatGPT 和 GPT-3 的区别 | The Difference Between ChatGPT and GPT-3
Items.Description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于如果您一直在关注，并且对这种技术的新演示 ChatGPT 和已经在各种环境中使用的 GPT-3 之间的区别感到困惑。

我向 ChatGPT 询问了这种差异，这就是它所说的：

它可以针对各种自然语言处理任务进行微调，包括语言翻译、文本摘要和问答。

另一方面，ChatGPT 是专门为聊天机器人应用程序设计的 GPT-3 模型的变体。 它已经在大型对话文本数据集上进行了训练，因此能够生成更适合在聊天机器人上下文中使用的响应。 在性能方面， ChatGPT 不如 GPT-3 强大，但它更适合聊天机器人应用。 它通常也比 GPT-3 更快、更高效，这使其成为在实时聊天机器人系统中使用的更好选择。 总体而言，ChatGPT 和 GPT-3 都是强大的语言模型，但它们的设计目的不同，各有优缺点。

重点是我自己。

原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on Dec 5

ChatGPT was announced a few days ago, and it has been a big topic of discussion in the tech world, for good reason.

In case you have been following along, and were confused about the difference between ChatGPT, which is a new demonstration of this type of technology, and GPT-3, which had already been in use in a variety of contexts.

I asked ChatGPT about that difference, and this is what it said:

ChatGPT and GPT-3 are both large language models trained by OpenAI, but they have some key differences. GPT-3, or Generative Pretrained Transformer 3, is the third generation of OpenAI's GPT language model, and it is one of the most powerful language models currently available. It can be fine-tuned for a wide range of natural language processing tasks, including language translation, text summarization, and question answering.

ChatGPT, on the other hand, is a variant of the GPT-3 model specifically designed for chatbot applications. It has been trained on a large dataset of conversational text, so it is able to generate responses that are more appropriate for use in a chatbot context. ChatGPT is also capable of inserting appropriate context-specific responses in conversations, making it more effective at maintaining a coherent conversation.

In terms of performance, ChatGPT is not as powerful as GPT-3, but it is better suited for chatbot applications. It is also generally faster and more efficient than GPT-3, which makes it a better choice for use in real-time chatbot systems. Overall, ChatGPT and GPT-3 are both powerful language models, but they are designed for different purposes and have different strengths and weaknesses.

Emphasis is my own.

Angelica Lo Duca - Dec 17 '21

Angelica Lo Duca - Nov 16 '21

Drew Ronsman - Dec 15 '21

  • 一个社会影响和慈善捐赠的平台，将捐助者与组织和事业联系起来，并提供影响衡量和报告的工具。

Items.Title: 使用 `git rebase` 将一个提交分成 2 个提交 | Split a commit into 2 commits with `git rebase`
Items.Description:
交互式变基允许您在分支中编辑、重新排序和拆分提交。 当您需要清理分支历史记录时，或者当您想要将包含对多个文件的更改的提交拆分为单独的提交时，这可能很有用。

您是否曾经不得不在项目中使用git rebase ，但在 pnpm/yarn/npm 锁文件中始终存在合并冲突？ 您可能听说过“始终在独立提交中提交锁定文件”的建议。

这是因为您可以在交互式变基中使用drop命令删除提交，这样您就可以在变基后重新生成锁定文件。

很多时候，我会忘记这一点，有时我会在一次提交中得到多个文件（包括我的锁文件），现在我无法drop它。

入门

在本教程中，我们将向您展示如何在交互式 git rebase 期间拆分提交。 我们将使用一个简单的示例来说明拆分提交所涉及的步骤。

首先，假设您有一个名为feature-branch ，该分支基于master分支。 功能分支中的Y提交包含对fileA和fileB的更改。 但是，您想将此提交拆分为两个单独的提交，一个用于fileA ，一个用于fileB 。

使用git rebase -i

为此，您需要使用git rebase -i命令启动交互式 rebase。 在此示例中，我们将使用 master 分支作为基础分支，因此命令为git rebase -i master 。

这将打开一个编辑器，您可以在其中指定要对功能分支中的每个提交执行的操作。 在这种情况下，您想要拆分Y提交，因此您需要将提交Y的pick命令替换为edit命令。

保存并关闭编辑器后，变基将开始，并在Y提交时停止。 这将允许您对Y提交进行更改。

拆分提交！

要拆分Y提交，您首先需要取消暂存提交中的更改。 这可以使用git reset HEAD~命令来完成。 这会将更改保留在您的工作目录中，但不会暂存。

接下来，您可以使用git add命令仅fileA的更改。 然后，您可以使用 git commit 命令创建一个包含fileA更改的新提交。 这将在feature-branch中创建一个新的提交Z ，如下所示：

 A - B - C - D [ master] / X - Y - Z [ feature-branch]

现在，您可以使用git add命令暂存fileB的剩余更改。 然后，您可以使用git commit命令创建一个包含fileB更改的新提交。 这将在feature-branch中创建一个新的提交W ，如下所示：

 A - B - C - D [ master] / X - Y - Z - W [ feature-branch]

多田！

最后，您可以使用git rebase --continue命令继续交互式变基。 这将应用您对Y提交所做的更改，并分别为fileA和fileB创建一个新的分支历史记录！

现在，只要你有一个讨厌的锁文件更改包含在提交中的其他文件中，你可以将它分离出来，然后在你的 rebase 期间drop它们，以使交互式 rebase 高效和干净！



原文:

Git is a powerful version control system that allows developers to track and manage changes to their code. One of the key features of Git is the ability to use interactive rebasing to modify the history of a branch.

Interactive rebasing allows you to edit, reorder, and split commits in a branch. This can be useful when you need to clean up your branch history, or when you want to split a commit that contains changes for multiple files into separate commits.

pnpm, yarn, npm lockfiles

Have you ever had to git rebase in your project but consistently had merge conflicts in your pnpm/yarn/npm lockfiles? You may have heard the advice to 'always commit your lockfile in an isolated commit'.

This is because you can use the drop command in an interactive rebase to remove the commit so you can regenerate the lockfile after your rebase.

Often times, I forget this and sometimes I end up with multiple files (including my lockfile) inside my single commit and now I cannot drop it.

Getting Started

In this tutorial, we will show you how to split a commit during an interactive git rebase. We will use a simple example to illustrate the steps involved in splitting a commit.

To begin, let's assume that you have a branch called feature-branch that is based on the master branch. The feature-branch contains three commits, X, Y, and Z, as shown below:

 A - B - C - D [master] / X - Y - Z [feature-branch]

The Y commit in the feature-branch contains changes for both fileA and fileB. However, you want to split this commit into two separate commits, one for fileA and one for fileB.

Using git rebase -i

To do this, you need to start an interactive rebase using the git rebase -i command. In this example, we will use the master branch as the base branch, so the command would be git rebase -i master.

This will open an editor where you can specify the actions that you want to perform on each commit in the feature-branch. In this case, you want to split the Y commit, so you need to replace the pick command for commit Y with the edit command.

Once you save and close the editor, the rebase will start and it will stop at the Y commit. This will allow you to make changes to the Y commit.

Split the Commit!

To split the Y commit, you first need to unstage the changes from the commit. This can be done using the git reset HEAD~ command. This will leave the changes in your working directory, but they will not be staged.

Next, you can use the git add command to stage only the changes for fileA. Then, you can use the git commit command to create a new commit with the changes for fileA. This will create a new commit, Z, in the feature-branch, as shown below:

 A - B - C - D [master] / X - Y - Z [feature-branch]

Now, you can use the git add command to stage the remaining changes for fileB. Then, you can use the git commit command to create a new commit with the changes for fileB. This will create a new commit, W, in the feature-branch, as shown below:

 A - B - C - D [master] / X - Y - Z - W [feature-branch]

Tada!

Finally, you can use the git rebase --continue command to continue the interactive rebase. This will apply the changes that you made to the Y commit and create a new branch history with the two separate commits for fileA and fileB respectively!

Now anytime you have a pesky lockfile change included with other files in a commit, you can separate it out, and then drop them during your rebases to make interactive rebase efficient and clean!

原文:

Uma das armadilhas mais comuns em muitas linguagens de programação, incluindo Java, é que acessar um membro de uma referência nula resultará em uma exceção de referência nula. Em Java, isso seria o equivalente a um NullPointerException ou NPE para abreviar.
O sistema de tipos do Kotlin visa eliminar NullPointerException do nosso código.
Kotlin fornece operadores Safe call(?.) , Elvis(?:), Not-null assertion(!!) para trabalhar em tipos anuláveis.

Safe Call(?.) Você pode usar o operador de chamada segura (?.) se não quiser lançar uma NullPointerException ao tentar acessar a referência anulável "safeCall"

val safeCall: String? = null
println(safeCall?.length)
  • Retorna null se for anulável e não lançará NullPointerException

Elvis Operator(?:) Quando temos uma referência anulável da variável "valor", podemos dizer “se 'valor' não for nulo, use-o, caso contrário, use algum valor não nulo”:

val valor: Int =
    if (valorVariavel != null) valorVariavel.length
    else -1

O código acima pode ser expresso com o Elvis Operator, escrito ?:

valorVariavel?.length ?: -1

Se a expressão à esquerda de ?: não for nula, o Elvis Operator a retorna, caso contrário, retorna a expressão à direita. Observe que a expressão do lado direito é avaliada somente se o lado esquerdo for nulo

Outro exemplo:

fun foo(node: Node): String? {
    val parent = node.getParent() ?: return null
    val name = node.getName() ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Exceção lançada")

    parent.getParent()?.getName()?.length

    return name
}

Not-null assertion operator(!!) No caso abaixo, se b for nulo, ocorrerá através da exceção de ponteiro nulo. Você pode usar (!!) se quiser lançar explicitamente uma exceção se o tipo for anulável.

val pergunta: String? = null
pergunta!!.toInt()

Items.Title: Svelte beats react：为您的下一个网络应用程序更快、更简单、更好！ | Svelte beats react: faster, simpler, and better for your next web app!
Items.Description:

这可以带来更好的用户体验和改进的性能。

这可以在使用 Svelte 构建应用程序时节省时间和精力。

这对于移动应用程序尤其重要。

这可以节省时间并改善整体开发体验。

所有这些优势使 Svelte 成为构建 Web 应用程序的引人注目的选择。 因此，如果您正在为您的下一个 Web 应用程序寻找更快、更简单、更好的框架，请考虑尝试一下 Svelte。

自己看

下面是一个简单的计数器组件如何在 React 和 Svelte 中实现的例子：

在反应中：

 import React , { useState } from ' react ' ; function Counter () { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); return ( < div > < p > { count } </ p > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count + 1 ) } > Increment </ button > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count - 1 ) } > Decrement </ button > </ div > ); }

苗条：

 <script> let count = 0 ; </script> <p> {count} </p> <button on:click= {() = > count++}>Increment </button> <button on:click= {() = > count--}>Decrement </button>

下面是一个简单的待办事项列表组件如何在 React 和 Svelte 中实现的示例：

反应：

 import React , { useState } from ' react ' ; function TodoList () { const [ todos , setTodos ] = useState ([]); const [ newTodo , setNewTodo ] = useState ( '' ); function handleAddTodo () { setTodos ([... todos , newTodo ]); setNewTodo ( '' ); } return ( < div > < ul > { todos . map (( todo , index ) => ( < li key = { index } > { todo } </ li > )) } </ ul > < input value = { newTodo } onChange = { ( event ) => setNewTodo ( event . target . value ) } /> < button onClick = { handleAddTodo } > Add Todo </ button > </ div > ); }

苗条：

 <script> let todos = []; let newTodo = '' ; function handleAddTodo () { todos = [... todos , newTodo ]; newTodo = '' ; } </script> <ul> {#each todos as todo} <li> {todo} </li> {/each} </ul> <input bind:value= {newTodo} /> <button on:click= {handleAddTodo} > Add Todo </button>

下面是一个更复杂的组件（例如登录表单）如何在 React 和 Svelte 中实现的示例：

反应：

 import React , { useState } from ' react ' ; import axios from ' axios ' ; function LoginForm () { const [ username , setUsername ] = useState ( '' ); const [ password , setPassword ] = useState ( '' ); const [ error , setError ] = useState ( null ); const [ loading , setLoading ] = useState ( false ); async function handleSubmit ( event ) { event . preventDefault (); setLoading ( true ); try { const response = await axios . post ( ' /login ' , { username , password }); const user = response . data ; // Save user to local storage, etc. } catch ( err ) { setError ( err . message ); } finally { setLoading ( false ); } } return ( < form onSubmit = { handleSubmit } > < label > Username: < input value = { username } onChange = { ( event ) => setUsername ( event . target . value ) } /> </ label > < label > Password: < input value = { password } onChange = { ( event ) => setPassword ( event . target . value ) } type = "password" /> </ label > { error && < p > { error } </ p > } < button disabled = { loading } > Login </ button > </ form > ); }

苗条：

 <script> import { post } from ' axios ' ; let username = '' ; let password = '' ; let error = null ; let loading = false ; async function handleSubmit ( event ) { event . preventDefault (); loading = true ; try { const { data : user } = await post ( ' /login ' , { username , password }); // Save user to local storage, etc. } catch ( err ) { error = err . message ; } finally { loading = false ; } } </script> <form on:submit | preventDefault= {handleSubmit} > <label> Username: <input bind:value= {username} /> </label> <label> Password: <input bind:value= {password} type= "password" /> </label> {#if error} <p> {error} </p> {/if} <button disabled= {loading} > Login </button> </form>

结论

总之，Svelte 是一个用于构建 Web 应用程序的强大而高效的 JavaScript 框架。 其处理状态和渲染组件的独特方法使其能够提供优于 React 等框架的多项优势，包括更好的性能、简单性和开发人员工具。 无论您是经验丰富的 Web 开发人员还是刚刚起步，Svelte 都值得您在下一个项目中考虑。 感谢您阅读。

原文:

Fersho Pls

Posted on Dec 6

Svelte is a relatively new JavaScript framework, but it has gained popularity for its simplicity and performance. Many people believe that Svelte is better than React for several reasons:

Svelte is faster than React. Svelte compiles your app's code at build time, which means that your app will run faster and use less memory at runtime. This can result in a better user experience and improved performance.

1. Svelte is easier to learn than React.

Svelte has a simpler and more intuitive syntax, which makes it easier for developers to pick up and start using. This can save time and effort when building apps with Svelte.

2. Svelte is smaller than React.

Svelte has a smaller codebase and produces smaller bundle sizes, which means that your app will be faster to download and use less storage on the user's device. This can be especially important for mobile apps.

3. Svelte has better developer tools than React.

Svelte includes a time-traveling debugger and other helpful features that make it easier for developers to find and fix bugs in their code. This can save time and improve the overall development experience.

All of these benefits make Svelte a compelling choice for building web applications. So if you're looking for a faster, simpler, and better framework for your next web app, consider giving Svelte a try.

See by yourself

Here is an example of how a simple counter component might be implemented in React and Svelte:

1. Creating a Counter

In react:

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <div> <p>{count}</p> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Increment</button> <button onClick={() => setCount(count - 1)}>Decrement</button> </div> );
}

in svelte:

<script> let count = 0;
</script> <p>{count}</p>
<button on:click={() => count++}>Increment</button>
<button on:click={() => count--}>Decrement</button>

2. Creating a TODO list

Here is an example of how a simple todo list component might be implemented in React and Svelte:

React:

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function TodoList() { const [todos, setTodos] = useState([]); const [newTodo, setNewTodo] = useState(''); function handleAddTodo() { setTodos([...todos, newTodo]); setNewTodo(''); } return ( <div> <ul> {todos.map((todo, index) => ( <li key={index}>{todo}</li> ))} </ul> <input value={newTodo} onChange={(event) => setNewTodo(event.target.value)} /> <button onClick={handleAddTodo}>Add Todo</button> </div> );
}

Svelte:

<script> let todos = []; let newTodo = ''; function handleAddTodo() { todos = [...todos, newTodo]; newTodo = ''; }
</script> <ul> {#each todos as todo} <li>{todo}</li> {/each}
</ul>
<input bind:value={newTodo} />
<button on:click={handleAddTodo}>Add Todo</button>

3. Complex component: Login Form

Here is an example of how a more complex component, such as a login form, might be implemented in React and Svelte:

React:

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import axios from 'axios'; function LoginForm() { const [username, setUsername] = useState(''); const [password, setPassword] = useState(''); const [error, setError] = useState(null); const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false); async function handleSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); setLoading(true); try { const response = await axios.post('/login', { username, password }); const user = response.data; // Save user to local storage, etc. } catch (err) { setError(err.message); } finally { setLoading(false); } } return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> <label> Username: <input value={username} onChange={(event) => setUsername(event.target.value)} /> </label> <label> Password: <input value={password} onChange={(event) => setPassword(event.target.value)} type="password" /> </label> {error && <p>{error}</p>} <button disabled={loading}>Login</button> </form> );
}

Svelte:

<script> import { post } from 'axios'; let username = ''; let password = ''; let error = null; let loading = false; async function handleSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); loading = true; try { const { data: user } = await post('/login', { username, password }); // Save user to local storage, etc. } catch (err) { error = err.message; } finally { loading = false; } }
</script> <form on:submit|preventDefault={handleSubmit}> <label> Username: <input bind:value={username} /> </label> <label> Password: <input bind:value={password} type="password" /> </label> {#if error} <p>{error}</p> {/if} <button disabled={loading}>Login</button>
</form>

Conclusion

In conclusion, Svelte is a powerful and efficient JavaScript framework for building web applications. Its unique approach to handling state and rendering components allows it to offer several advantages over frameworks like React, including better performance, simplicity, and developer tools. Whether you're a seasoned web developer or just getting started, Svelte is worth considering for your next project. Thank you for reading.

Items.Title: 在 docker 上运行 gitlab runner | running a gitlab runner on docker
Items.Description:
在 Windows 机器上的 Git Bash 中运行以下命令：

  1. 运行docker pull gitlab/gitlab-runner ：这将拉取在 Docker Hub 上找到的 Gitlab Runner 图像。 由Gitlab自己管理

  2. 运行此命令：

 docker run -d --name gitlab-runner --restart always \ -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner \ -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest

它基于我们在第一步中拉取的 Gitlab runner 镜像创建一个容器，并将容器中的某些配置文件挂载到本地系统卷，以便它们在容器重启时持久存在（如果我正在运行容器，可能非常有用在我的本地机器上，因为我会经常打开和关闭那台电脑）

  1. 运行此命令以注册跑步者： winpty docker run --rm -it -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner gitlab/gitlab-runner register

  2. 我传递给交互式 CLI 的内容：

  3. 注册令牌（也可以在Settings > CI/CD > Runners下找到）

  4. 跑步者描述（本地码头集装箱跑步者）

  5. 标签（调试、设置）

  6. 可选维护说明（只需按 Enter 即可留空）

  7. 执行者（码头工人）

  8. 默认 Docker 镜像 (gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest)

实际上，我不确定我是否能做到。 让我再次尝试运行管道，看看它现在是否可以执行，因为我有一个运行器设置 - 哦，它正在运行 - 没关系，它仍然卡住了

哦这很有启发性： https ://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/runners/configure_runners.html#set-a-runner-to-run-untagged-jobs

原文:

running the following commands in Git Bash on a Windows machine:

  1. run docker pull gitlab/gitlab-runner: this pulls the Gitlab Runner image found on Docker Hub. it's managed by Gitlab themselves

  2. run this command:

docker run -d --name gitlab-runner --restart always \
  -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner \
  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
  gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest

it creates a container based on the Gitlab runner image we pulled in step one, and it mounts certain configuration files in the container to the local system volume so that they persist upon restart of the container (probably super useful if i'm running the container on my local machine, since i'll be turning that computer on and off a lot)

  1. run this command to register the runner: winpty docker run --rm -it -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner gitlab/gitlab-runner register

  2. original instructions on Gitlab docs don't have the winpty, i added it after Git Bash told me it wasn't a TTY and that I should try adding it

  3. what i passed to the interactive CLI:

  4. gitlab instance url (found under Settings > CI/CD > Runners)

  5. registration token (also found under Settings > CI/CD > Runners)

  6. runner description (local docker container runner)

  7. tags (debug, setup)

  8. optional maintenance note (left blank by simply hitting Enter)

  9. executor (docker)

  10. default Docker image (gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest)

Items.Title: AWS Community Summit APAC & Japan 2022 的美好回忆 | Stunning memories from AWS Community Summit APAC & Japan 2022
Items.Description:
发表于这 2 天绝对是一个家外之家，计划周密，组织严密，执行完美。 就个人而言，对于多次前往曼谷并且每次都渴望素食的人来说，预先计划的素食非常美味。

目录

欢迎晚宴

沙夫拉兹对这次聚会表示衷心的欢迎。 与我们一直通过电话和视频通话互动的社区人士见面和打招呼确实令人耳目一新。

塞拉娜 (Serana) 让观众参与到深思熟虑的终极测验中。 为前 3 名获奖者提供的 50 美元亚马逊代金券保持了高能量和高参与度。

社区奖

印度在所有 10 个奖项类别的多项提名中获得 3 项重大胜利。

这是他对社区的巨大贡献

这就是为什么陪审团会认为 Vivek 是该奖项的正确选择。

  • 他与 Neetu Mallan 最近推出了 AWS Cloud Practitioner Bootcamp，其中有 17 个视频课程、2000+ 次观看和 500+ 次注册，其中 400 名用户是 AWS 的首次用户。
  • 他还致力于新计划，例如 EverydayAWS（每天提供一项 AWS 服务）、AWS Quidditch（一种通过测验学习和理解 AWS 技术的游戏化方式）、AWS Comics（帮助非技术人员和初学者学习和理解 AWS 服务通过漫画）和 AWS Memes（通过社交媒体吸引特别关注泰米尔语的人）。

从我的眼里，

作为来自印度的最年轻的 AWS 英雄，Vivek 展示了更成熟的方式来运行 AWS 社区聚会，为首次演讲者提供支持，创建一个平台以通过本地语言#Tamil 学习 AWS 云并运行各种计划，“Invent and Simply”类别奖项听起来是为 Vivek Raja PS 定制的 👏 👏 👏
祝 Vivek Raja PS、Neetu Mallan 和 AWS 用户组 Madurai 获得更多荣誉💐

我继续叙述什么会获得AWS 年度英雄的认可，以下是我想到的几件事

  • 首先，我将功劳归功于我的男性盟友 Jeevan Dongre、Sathyajith Bhat、Runcy Oomen、Prashanth HN 和 Habeeb，他们为我创建了一个更具包容性的平台，让我可以在展示技术讲座、组织聚会、组织和举办 AWS 社区日方面做自己事件，写博客等等。
  • 有幸连续 3 年（2019 年、2020 年和 2021 年）出席 AWS reInvent 大会并为此感到自豪
  • 有史以来第一位在 AWS 印度峰会上发表主题演讲的 AWS 精英，以及 Ekta Parashar 和其他 AWS 客户。 在 AWS 印度最大的技术活动中获得这个机会和代表权不仅对我自己而且对社区来说都是一个巨大的胜利。
  • 有机会在 AWS 上的基础设施即代码大规模端到端 CI/CD印度峰会上举办分组会议吗
  • 通过 AWS 会议、3P 社区、行业会议和大学享受撰写博客、媒体文章和介绍 AWS 和 DevTools 的乐趣。
  • 在 AWS 认证、博客、社区连接等方面指导早期开发人员或云有志者，获得满足感。
  • 最近在 TedEx for Youth 上作为一名技术爱好者在我的旅程中发表演讲，同时也代表 AWS 社区。
  • 与 AWS 团队合作制作 AWS 开发者故事，以激励更多女性拥抱科技领域，我们一起非常成功地获得了 270 万次观看。

好吧，我想，它也值得拥有一个自己的职位。

社区告别 Rohini

短幻灯片

好吧，一如既往，我的活动博客以大量图片和描述而闻名。 为了改变，图片变成了带有轻音乐的幻灯片式视频，我喜欢它:)

通常一个活动会有一两个博客和视频，但即使在 10 天后，我们仍然会继续看到来自体验过并喜欢该活动的代表的新博客和视频。 很想把他们都拍在一个屋檐下

博客

影片

  • 谢谢你

    非常感谢背后的 AWS 团队，是他们让 2022 年首届印度 AWS 社区峰会成为我们所有人难忘的一届。
    他们不仅让我们的曼谷之行成为一次难忘的旅行，还确保我们平安回家。 我肯定会引用一些 Shafraz、Ridhima 和 Rohini 以他们的关怀和热情脱颖而出的事件。

    一

    尽管所有 AWS 社区峰会与会者都通过电子邮件和 Slack 进行了大量交流。 我们有很多第一次来曼谷的游客，Rohini 非常耐心地通过她的个人经历和过去的访问来解决所有这些问题，这对很多人来说都很方便。

    二

    我讨厌一大早就去机场。 当我得知 Shafraz 也将乘坐同一航班时，我们决定在早上 5:30 左右一起离开去机场。 后来他告诉我他要提前一个小时离开，因为他有一些工作，而且他很清楚我是自由行。 到达机场后，我得知他提前出差陪同另一位 AWS 社区峰会代表前往机场帮助她。 对此深表敬意！！

    三

    另一位来自 AWS 社区峰会的代表错过了前往曼谷的转机航班，而是另行安排抵达。 正准备办理登机手续时，她发现由于错过了续程航班，回程机票也被取消了。

    她给 Shafraz 打了电话，但他已经办理了登机手续，完成了安检，马上就要起飞了，所以他不能回去帮助她。 我们都开始与来泰国帮助预订同一航班机票的 Neoniche 团队协调。 为了订到同一个航班的机票，我们开始和前来泰国的Neoniche团队协调。 自 2018 年以来，Neoniche 团队一直在处理我与 AWS 相关的旅行，因此我与他们的印度有联系。 我们一大早就把他们叫醒，他们也很热心的帮我们联系他们的旅行伙伴，帮我们买票。 但是那张票无法在线预订。 这时候，洛希妮和莉蒂玛也加入了谈话，她们也在探讨着不同的选择。 最后，女强人Ridhima赶到机场为我们的朋友取了同一航班的机票并送她回家。

    这不是赞助或邀请某人参加活动，而是确保个人的安全和福祉，直到他们安全回家才有意义。 我衷心感谢整个团队！

    千万不要错过这个可爱的视频，以了解为此付出的努力以及该活动在#awscommunity 中产生的影响

    大卫德保利斯 - 一旦暂停，aws-heroes 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，aws-heroes 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，aws-heroes 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 aws-heroes 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 aws-heroes：

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 aws-heroes 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Bhuvaneswari Subramani for AWS Heroes

Posted on Dec 6

AWS Community Summit APAC & Japan 2022 was first of its kind inviting 300+ AWS Community Builders, AWS Community Leads, and AWS Heroes from countries across APAC & Japan.

That 2 days was certainly a home away from home, very well planned, organized and flawlessly executed. Personally for someone who have made several trips to Bangkok and every-time end up craving for vegetarian food, a pre-planned vegetarian meals were delicious.

Table of Contents

Welcome Dinner

Shafraz whole heartedly welcomed the gathering. It was indeed refreshing to meet and greet community folks with whom we all have been interacting over phone and video calls.

Serana kept the audience engaged with the well thought Ultimate Quiz. The $50 amazon vouchers for the top 3 winners kept the energy and participation high.

Community Awards

India had 3 big wins over and above several nominations across all 10 award category.

Sanchit Jain for "Earn Trust" Community Award

Sanchit Jain is someone who have earned trust as a doer even before he technically became the co-organizer for AWS User Group Mumbai.
Here is his great contribution to the community

Vivek Raja for "Invent and Simplify" Community Award

Here is why Juries would have thought Vivek is the right choice for the award.

  • Vivek  launched AWS Madurai UG in February 2022, and has been continuously innovating new ways to engage the community.
  • UG launch event on YouTube saw 550+ views, highest among any UG in India and South Asia. He along with Neetu Mallan recently launched the AWS Cloud Practitioner Bootcamp, which featured 17 video sessions, 2K+ views and 500+ registrations, out of which 400 users were first time AWS users.
  • He is also working on new initiatives like EverydayAWS (features one AWS service each day), AWS Quidditch (A gamified way of learning and understanding AWS tech through Quiz), AWS Comics (To help non-tech and beginners to learn and understand AWS services through comics) and AWS Memes (to onboard people from social media especially focussed on Tamil language).

From my eyes,

Being the youngest AWS Hero from India, Vivek have shown more mature ways of running AWS community meetups, empowering first-time speakers, creating a platform to learn aws cloud through local language, #Tamil and running various initiatives, "Invent and Simply" category award sounded custom-made for Vivek Raja P S 👏 👏 👏
Wishing you many more laurels to Vivek Raja P S, Neetu Mallan and AWS User Group Madurai💐

Bhuvaneswari Subramani as "AWS Hero of the Year" Community Award

I went on recounting what would have fetched AWS Hero of the Year recognition, here are few things which comes to my mind

  • First and foremost I attribute the credit to my male allies Jeevan Dongre, Sathyajith Bhat, Runcy Oomen, Prashanth HN and Habeeb for creating a more inclusive platform for me to be myself in terms presenting tech talks, organizing meetups, organizing and hosting AWS Community day events, writing blogs and so on.
  • Had the pleasure and proud privilege of presenting at AWS reInvent in 3 consecutive years - 2019, 2020, and 2021
  • First-ever AWS Hero to speak at the AWS Summit India keynote, along with Ekta Parashar and other AWS customers. Receiving this opportunity and representation in AWS India's largest tech event was a great win not just for myself but for the community.
  • Had the great opportunity presented a breakout session for the India Summit on End-to-End CI/CD at scale with Infrastructure-as-Code on AWS
  • Enjoy writing blogs, media articles and present about AWS and DevTools via AWS conferences, 3P communities, Industry Conferences and universities.
  • Get the contentment in guiding early developers or cloud aspirants on AWS certification, blogging, community connect etc.
  • Recently got to speak at TedEx for Youth on my journey as a technophile and represented AWS community too.
  • Got to work with AWS Team for AWS Developer story to inspire more women to embrace tech space and together we were quite successful to fetch 2.7 million views.

Well, I thought, it warrants a post of its own too.

Community Farewell to Rohini

Short SlideShow

Well, as always my event blogs are know for lots of pics and descriptions. For change, pictures turned into a slideshow kind of video with lil music, which I loved :)

Blogs & Videos on AWS APAC & Japan Community Summit

Usually an event will have one or two blogs and videos but even after 10 days, we still continue to see new blogs and videos from the delegates who have experienced and enjoyed the event. Would love to capture them all under one roof

Blogs

Videos

  • AWS APAC Community Summit'22 - Experience with AWS UG Madurai by Vivek Raja P S

  • Moments from APAC & Japan AWS Community Summit 2022 by Jones Zachariah Noel

Thank You

A big Thank you to the AWS Team behind, who made this first ever AWS Community Summit India 2022 a memorable one for all of us.
They not only made our visit to Bangkok a memorable one but also ensured that we are back home safe and sound. I would definitely quote few incidents where Shafraz, Ridhima and Rohini stood out with their care and warmth.

One

Though lots of communication happened to all AWS Community Summit attendees through emails and slack. We had lots of first time travellers to Bangkok, Rohini was very patient in addressing all of that through her personal experience and past visits which was handy for many.

Two

I hate to travel early morning to airport. When I learnt Shafraz also going to be in same flight and we decided to leave together to airport around 5:30am. Later he informed me that he has to leave an hour early because he has some work and he knows very well that I am independent traveller. Upon reaching airport, I learnt that he advanced his travel to accompany another delegate of AWS Community Summit to airport to help her out. Huge respect for that!!

Three

Another delegate from AWS Community Summit missed the onward flight to Bangkok and arrived with an alternate arrangement. As she was about to check in, she realized that the return flight ticket had also been cancelled since she missed the onward flight.

She called Shafraz but he has checked in, finished security, and is about to fly so he can't go back and help her. We both started coordinating with Neoniche team who had come to Thailand to help book the ticket on the same flight. In order to book the ticket on the same flight, we began coordinating with the Neoniche team that had come to Thailand. The Neoniche team has been working on my AWS-related travel since 2018, hence I had their India contacts. We woke them up early in the morning and they were also eager to help us contact their travel partner and purchase a ticket. But that ticket wasn't available to book online. By now, Rohini and Ridhima had joined in the conversation, and they were also exploring different options. Finally, the superwoman Ridhima, rushed to the airport to get the ticket for our friend in the same flight and sent her home.

Its not about sponsoring or inviting someone for event but ensuring the safety and well being of the individual until they are back home safe means a lot. My sincere appreciation to the entire team!

Never miss this lovely video to get a glimpse of the effort that has gone into it and the impact the event have made in the #awscommunity

Davide de Paolis - Nov 17

Items.Title: AWS re:Invent 2022 变成了庆祝活动！ | AWS re:Invent 2022 turned into a time for Celebration!
Items.Description:
这是我第 4 次亲自参与 re:Invent，很高兴能参加 5 场不同的演讲活动，包括一场演讲、一场小组讨论和 3 场采访。

但那种兴奋变成了惊喜和荣誉。 我非常高兴能够获得亚马逊首席技术官 Werner Vogels 博士颁发的 Now Go Build 2022 奖。

目录

登记

在每个大的里程碑上，我都用母语泰米尔语重申自己“ tamil” tamil“என்னிலைதன்னிலைஇனித்தொருவேண்டும்，இறைவா！”
这意味着，在前进和成功的每一步，我都寻求全能者的祝福，以保持谦虚和脚踏实地。

成为 AWS 英雄本身就是一种荣誉，我非常高兴能够从 Dr.Werner Vogels 那里获得著名的 Now Go Build 奖，以表彰我对 AWS 社区的模范贡献。

社交媒体链接：
什么是 Now Go Build Award？
每年，Amazon 首席技术官 Werner Vogels 博士都会颁发 Now Go Build 奖，以表彰其中一位在各自领域备受尊重并表现出强烈社区参与的 AWS 英雄。 获奖者是创新者，他们超越自我，激励他人，并作为榜样在技术世界产生有意义的影响。

而今年，Werner 将 NGB 奖颁给了你们！！ 表彰我在 AWS 社区中的领导地位，以及通过演讲、指导、用户组活动等与他人分享知识的不懈努力。

在来自 53 个国家/地区的 252 名 AWS 英雄中，我非常自豪能够在 AWS 英雄私人晚宴上获得今年的 Go Build 奖。 事实上，我是世界上第二位，也是第一位获此殊荣的女性。 哇，多么好的开始 re:Invent 的方式？
我要感谢 AWS 用户组班加罗尔和 AWS 用户组印度。

我真的无话可说，但我确实要感谢来自 AWS、社区和同事的一大堆朋友。

首先，它始终是 AWS 用户组班加罗尔的组织者——Jeevan Dongre、Sathyajith Bhat、Prashanth HN 和 Runcy Oommen，他们自 2017 年以来一直支持我。

我总是感谢 Ross Barich、Ridhima Kapoor、Rohini Ganoekar、Suman Debnath、Rashmi、Shafraz、David、Ernesto 等。

我们总是对深思熟虑的 SWAG 感到兴奋，它就在这里。

重新：发明 AWS UG Lightning

感谢 Karissa 尽最大努力将我们聚集在一个屋檐下，提供了一个关于社区最佳实践和成功案例的知识共享平台。

我有幸在 re:Invent 的 AWS 用户组会议上向全球 AWS 用户组领导发表讲话，讨论印度的 AWS 社区如何反映多样性中的统一性
印度是遍布 28 个州的文化和遗产的象征， 24 个 AWS 用户组使用 22 种不同的语言，横跨从北到南和从东到西。 我有机会从不同的角度谈论我们如何团结一致并相互支持以举办聚会、举办 AWS 社区日、作为演讲者待命以及向四周传播积极的氛围。 在包容性社区方面，为早期开发人员提供创建平台，WomenInTech 参与在过去 5 年中如何在印度 AWS 社区中恶化。

幻灯片—— 这里

云端职业之旅

从事技术职业有很多途径。 找到你擅长的事情和让你“打勾”的事情会有很长的路要走。 我很高兴与美国的 Ivonne Roberts 和英国的 Chloe McAteer 一起参加由 AWS 学术宣传负责人 Jen Looper 主持的小组讨论，我们在会上谈到了我们独特的职业旅程，这些旅程为人们如何驾驭他们的职业旅程提供了实用和鼓舞人心的见解。 探索弹性，确定您的优势，继续学习什么，甚至我们在本次小组讨论中讨论的如何继续学习。

我很高兴能参与录制视频，其中 AWS 英雄社区成员 (Vanessa 和 Brian Tarbox) 就思想领导力和指导主题相互采访，旨在让社区拥有对话的所有权，鼓励代表性不足的新手建设者进入领域和社区一起建设得更好。

我们得讨论一下

  • 用户组/社区学习
  • 克服进入技术的障碍/途径
  • 真实生活经历：学校没有教给你的东西
  • 技术向善/工具的力量和可能性
  • 云中的各种角色以及对他们有用的软技能（即 AI 中的同理心、解决问题的创造性思维）
  • 职业旅程和建议

原文:

Pramod Dutta

Posted on Dec 6

Question 1 : What is Protractor?
Answers : Protractor is an end-to-end test framework for Angular and AngularJS applications.

Protractor runs tests against your application running in a real browser, interacting with it as a user would.

Question 2 : Can Protractor be used for testing Non-Angular JS Applications? Yes. Protractor can be used for testing Non-Angular JS or Non-Angular Apps. browser.ignoreSynchronization = true;

browser.waitforangularenabled(false)

Question 3 : What are the test frameworks supported by Protractor?
Protractor supports two behavior driven development (BDD) test frameworks such as Jasmine & Mocha.

Question 4 : What is a conf file in Protractor? The configuration file tells Protractor how to set up the Selenium Server, which tests to run, how to set up the browsers, and which test framework to use. The configuration file can also include one or more global settings.

The config file provides explanations for all of the Protractor configuration options. Default settings include the standalone Selenium Server, the Chrome browser, and the Jasmine test framework.

Question 5 : What is a spec file in Protractor?

Spec file is the one where we write actual test code. It contains the logic and locators to interact with an application.

Question 6 : How to exclude a spec file in Protractor? You can exclude the spec’s by adding them in the exclude tag in your conf.js file. You can also add patterns to exclude many test scripts with similar names. Sometimes we may face a situation of running only few tests out of all. In this case, we use exclude tag in our conf.js file to run only few tests. Say we want to exclude test name “myTest.js”

exclude: [myTest.js]

Question 7 : What are the locators in Protractor?
It has unique set of locators particularly to identify elements based on AngularJS attributes.

by.className by.css by.id by.linkText by.name by.partialLinkText by.tagName

by.xpath

Question 9 : How to add custom locator for Protractor?
By using addLocator method.

Question 10 : How do you check the status of a webdriver manager and how do you update webdriver manager?
To check the status of webdriver manager, run the following in your command prompt

webdriver-manager status

To update webdriver manager, run the following code in your command prompt

webdriver-manager update

Question 11 : What is browser’s command in Protractor?
driver.navigate().refresh();

browser.refresh makes a full reload of the current page and loads mock modules before Angular.

Question 12 : What is the difference between “GET” and “NAVIGATE in Protractor? GET: Get method is used to navigate to the given destination.

browser.get(“https://www.softwaretestingmaterial.com

”); Navigate: Navigate method is used to navigate to the given destination browser.navigate()

If we want to load a previous page or next page in a browser we can use back and forward.

browser.navigate().back();
browser.navigate().forward();

Question 13 : How to accept/cancel Alert in Protractor?
By using accept() method of Alerts class, we accept Alerts in Protractor.

Note: We have to switch the control to the alert using switchTo().alert() before accepting the alert.

browser.switchTo().alert().accept()

browser.switchTo().alert().dismiss()

Question 14 : How do you stop Page Loading in Protractor when an element is loaded?
By using Key.ESC to body element in Protractor.

browser.actions().sendKeys(protractor.Key.ESC).perform();
browser.actions().sendKeys(protractor.Key.ESC).perform();

Question 15 : What are the browsers supported by Protractor?
Chrome, firefox, opera, IE,Safari

Items.Title: 什么是开发人员生产力以及如何衡量它？ | What is developer productivity and how should it be measured?
Items.Description:
开发人员的生产力非常简单。 它是关于开发人员如何在有限的时间内高效开发的。 这也可以基于项目在给定时间的团队生产水平。 当组织或团队希望跟踪进度、确定资源、开发基准或只是授予开发人员最高生产力时，此类问题就变得很重要。

但对于许多公司、团队或管理者来说，决定使用哪种指标来衡量生产力一直很困难。

为什么很难衡量发展生产力？

在衡量组织的生产力时，要考虑必须考虑的指标数量并不容易。 例如，个人和团队的生产力可能看起来相似，甚至被视为同一件事。 然而，个人的高生产力并不一定意味着团队的生产力或团队的出色生产并不一定等于每个人令人印象深刻的生产力。

因此，在考察一个团队或个人的工作效率之前，一个先行的措施是先就生产力的定义达成一致，并明确为什么需要测量生产力。 有人可能会说，生产力就是充分利用有限的时间。 它可能是几周内提交的次数或已修复错误的数量。 任何事情都可以成为生产力的衡量标准。 因此，重要的是要了解我们为什么需要它以及我们将如何定义它。

我们应该如何衡量它？

在开始任何生产力测量之前，我们必须问问自己。 在团队中的每个人都能够理解并同意为什么需要这样的测量之前，不建议继续进行下去。 同样重要的是要提醒自己，生产力并不一定等于绩效。

开车时，最短的时间并不总是找到最佳路线的答案。 最短的路线可能是在一条危险的道路中间，那里有悬崖，可能会危及您的生命。 找到最佳的行车路线或生产力是优化的路线，而不是最短或最快的路线。 因此，只有一个指标不能成为一切的北极星。 考虑实现目标的各种措施非常重要。

生产力衡量的一些例子是什么？

进度时间

尽管跟踪时间似乎有毒，但这并不一定意味着要在最后一分钟跟踪工作时间。 这只是为了跟踪我们在工作时花费最多的时间，并检查时间分配是否足够高效。 通常，很容易被卷入某些项目而忽略最初的计划。 通过跟踪大部分时间耗尽的地方，找到一种不同的方法来提高工作流程效率可能是提高生产力的另一种方法。 例如， FUNCTION12是一种面向前端开发人员的设计到代码自动化工具，可帮助生成带有 Figma 链接的代码。 通过引入此类工具，可以在不改变开发团队中的任何工作流程的情况下显着提高开发效率。

完成的任务

根据已完成任务的数量来衡量生产力可能不是最理想的方法。 然而，考虑到每项任务的复杂性，完成任务的数量可以作为评估或提高生产力的衡量标准。 例如，上周完成10个复杂任务和本周完成50个普通任务，因为任务难度不同，无法比较。 但是，通过比较具有相似复杂程度的已完成任务并考虑通常在每个项目上花费多少时间，您或团队可以计划如何在一周内分配任务以提高工作效率。

结论

努力工作高效，产出更好，是每个开发者都希望提高的。 但是，了解并区分生产力和性能之间的差异非常重要。 有人可能会根据在一个项目上花费了多少时间来评估和评价我们的表现。 但是生产力应该是为了我们自己。 不是为了工作更多，而是为了工作得更好。

原文:

Developer productivity is quite simple. It’s about how a developer can efficiently develop in a limited amount of time. This can also be based on the team’s production level at a given time for a project. Such a matter comes to importance when an organization or team wishes to track progress, determine resources, develop benchmarks, or simply grant the developer the highest productivity.

But it has always been difficult for many companies, teams, or managers to decide on which metric to use for productivity measurement.

Why is it hard to measure development productivity?

When measuring productivity in an organization, it cannot be easy regarding the number of metrics that must be considered. For example, the productivity of an individual and a team may seem similar or even regarded as the same matter. However, an individual’s high productivity doesn’t necessarily mean a team’s productivity or a team’s great production doesn’t necessarily equal every individual’s impressive productivity.

Therefore, before checking the work efficiency of a team or an individual, a prior measure is rather agreeing on a definition of productivity, and setting an aim on why productivity measurement is required must be started first. Some might say productivity is making the most out of the limited time. It might be how many commits were made in a matter of weeks or the number of fixed bugs. Anything and everything can be the metrics for productivity. Therefore, it’s important to learn why we need this and how we’re going to define it.

How should we measure it?

1. Setting a goal

Before starting any productivity measurement, we must ask ourselves. “Why do we need this?” Before everyone in the team can understand and agree with why such measurement is required, it’s not recommended to go on further. It’s also important to remind ourselves that productivity doesn’t necessarily equal performance.

2. Finding the right metric

When driving, the shortest time isn’t always the answer to finding the best route. The shortest route might be in the middle of a dangerous road with a cliff that can risk your life. Finding the best driving route or productivity is an optimized route, never the shortest or fastest. Therefore, only one metric can’t be the Northstar for everything. Considering various measures to achieve your goal is rather important.

What are some examples of productivity measurement?

Progress time

Although tracking time may seem toxic, this doesn't necessarily mean tracking the working time by the last minute. This is only to track where our time is the most spent when working and check if the time distribution is productive enough. Often, it’s easy to be swept away into certain projects and ignore the original plans. By tracking where most time is exhausted, finding a different approach to make workflow efficient can be another way to enhance productivity. For example, FUNCTION12, a design-to-code automation tool for front-end developers helps produce codes with a Figma link. By introducing such tools, development productivity can be increased drastically without changing any workflow in the development team.

Completed tasks

Measuring productivity on the number of completed tasks may not be the most desirable approach. However, with the consideration of each task’s complexity, the number of completed tasks can be a measure to assess or enhance productivity. For example, completing 10 complex tasks last week and 50 common tasks this week can’t be compared since the difficulty of the tasks is different. However, by comparing the completed tasks with a similar level of complexity and considering how much time is usually spent on each project, you or the team can plan out how to distribute the tasks throughout the week to work with the most productivity.

Conclusion

Striving to work efficiently and produce better is what every developer wishes to improve. However, it’s important to understand and separate the difference between productivity and performance. Someone may assess and evaluate our performance based on how many hours were spent on a project. But productivity should be for ourselves. Not to work more, but rather, to work better.

Items.Title: 用简单的英语解释 JavaScript 回调函数 | JavaScript callback functions explained in plain English
Items.Description:
吉奥穆卡斯

发表于在简单的语言中，回调函数只是将另一个函数作为参数的函数。 而已。

让我们举一个小例子来更好地说明我在说什么。

 function calc(add){ let a = add + 3; console.log(a); } const add = (a,b) => { let sum = a + b; return sum; } calc(add(2,4));

这里我有两个功能

计算并添加

先说加法吧。
这里的add函数就是回调函数。 我们在这里调用 add 作为另一个函数内的参数。 这符合回调函数的定义。

我们还可以将匿名函数作为回调函数传递。

 function sayHello(hi){ console.log('Hello'); hi(); } sayHello(function(){ console.log('hi') }) Output: hello hi

我们经常在不知情的情况下使用回调。 考虑 setTimeout 函数。

这里 setTimeout 打印“Inside a callback”。 这也是一个匿名回调函数。

想象一下如果回调不存在。 没有它，我们将无法进行异步行为。

原文:

Geo Mukkath

Posted on Dec 6

In simple language Callback functions are just functions that take another function as it’s argument. That’s it.

Let’s take a small example to better demonstrate what I am talking about.

function calc(add){
    let a = add + 3;
  console.log(a);
}

const add = (a,b) => {
    let sum = a + b;
  return sum;
}

calc(add(2,4));

Here I have two functions

calc and add

Let’s first talk about add.
The add function adds two values and returns the value.

The calc function takes an argument and whatever the argument returns, it adds 3 to it.

The add function over here is the callback function. We are calling add here as a parameter inside another function. This fits the the definition of callback functions.

We can also pass anonymous functions as callback functions.

function sayHello(hi){
    console.log('Hello');
  hi();
}

sayHello(function(){
 console.log('hi')
})

Output: 
hello
hi

sayHello passes an anonymous function.

We use callbacks often without even knowing it. Consider the setTimeout function.

setTimeout uses the callback function like so

setTimeout(() => {console.log('Inside a callback')},3000);
// logs 'Inside a callback' after 3 seconds

Here setTimeout prints ‘Inside a callback’. This too was a callback function that too an anonymous one.

Imagine if callback didn’t exists. We wouldn’t be able to make asynchronous behavior without it.

原文:

Santa Claus necesita hacer una revisión de sus cajas de regalos para asegurarse de que puede empaquetarlas todas en su trineo. Cuenta con una serie de cajas de diferentes tamaños, que se caracterizan por su longitud, anchura y altura.

Tu tarea es escribir una función que, dada una lista de cajas con sus tamaños, determine si es posible empaquetar todas las cajas en una sola de manera que cada caja contenga a otra (que a su vez contenga a otra, y así sucesivamente).

Cada caja representa sus medidas con un objeto. Por ejemplo: {l: 2, w: 3, h: 2}. Esto significa que la caja tiene una longitud de 2, una anchura de 3 y una altura de 2.

Una caja entra en otra caja si todos los lados de la primera son menores a los lados de la segunda. Los elfos nos han dicho que las cajas no se pueden rotar, así que no se puede poner una caja de 2x3x2 en una caja de 3x2x2. Veamos unos ejemplos:

fitsInOneBox([ { l: 1, w: 1, h: 1 }, { l: 2, w: 2, h: 2 }
]) // true

En el ejemplo anterior, la caja más pequeña entra en la caja más grande. Por lo tanto, es posible empaquetar todas las cajas en una sola. Ahora veamos un caso que no:

const boxes = [ { l: 1, w: 1, h: 1 }, { l: 2, w: 2, h: 2 }, { l: 3, w: 1, h: 3 }
] fitsInOneBox(boxes) // false

En el ejemplo anterior, la caja más pequeña entra en la caja del medio, pero la caja del medio no entra en la caja más grande. Por lo tanto, no es posible empaquetar todas las cajas en una sola.

Ten en cuenta que las cajas pueden no venir en orden:

const boxes = [ { l: 1, w: 1, h: 1 }, { l: 3, w: 3, h: 3 }, { l: 2, w: 2, h: 2 }
] fitsInOneBox(boxes) // true

En el ejemplo anterior, la primer caja cabe en la tercera, y la tercera en la segunda. Por lo tanto, es posible empaquetar todas las cajas en una sola.

Cosas a tener en cuenta:

Las cajas no se pueden rotar ya que los elfos nos han dicho que la máquina no está preparada. Las cajas pueden venir desordenadas de tamaño.

Las cajas no son siempre cuadradas, pueden ser rectangulares.

Items.Title: 我的数组迭代方法之旅 | My Journey Through Array Iteration Methods
Items.Description:
数组迭代方法是我在 Flatiron School 期间必须掌握的最难的概念。 事实上，它们中有很多也使得学习它们不是一件容易的事。 然而，随着时间的推移，您可以理解它们，并在您自己的项目中利用它们的功能！ 使用它们一段时间后，我有信心逐步了解每种迭代方法可用于的基本任务！

他们都将回调函数作为参数。 两者的主要区别是.find()只返回第一个返回真值的元素给回调函数，而.filter()返回所有返回真值的元素给回调函数。

 const arr = [ 1 , 2 , 7 , 45 , 63 ] const found = arr . find ( element => element > 5 ) console . log ( found ) //output: 7
 cosnt arr = [ 1 , 2 , 7 , 45 , 63 ] const found = arr . filter ( element => element > 5 ) console . log ( found ) //output: [7, 45, 63]

第一个代码示例展示了 .find() 如何处理一个简单的数字数组。 虽然 45 和 63 也大于 5，但 .find() 方法只会返回第一个真值，即 7。另一方面，.filter() 将返回 7、45 和 63，因为它们都是真值在这种情况下。 重要的是要注意 .filter() 返回新数组中的值。

它将对数组中的每个项目调用一次回调函数，并返回一个新的变异值数组。

 const arr = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ] const newArr = arr . map ( element => element * 5 ) console . log ( newArr ) //output: [5, 10, 15, 20, 25]

如果需要，.map() 也可以用作数组复制方法。

 const arr = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ] const newArr = arr . map ( element => element ) console . log ( newArr ) //output: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

这两个示例都展示了 .map() 方法的基本功能，但 .map() 用途广泛，可用于许多不同的情况。 这是我编写的一段代码，用于将字符串数组中每个单词的第一个字母大写！ 你会看到你甚至可以通过在另一个 .map() 中调用它来链接 .map()

 const titleCased = () => { return tutorials . map ( function ( string ){ let word = string . split ( ' ' ) let capWords = word . map ( function ( word ){ return word . charAt ( 0 ). toUpperCase () + word . substring ( 1 ) }) return capWords . join ( ' ' ) }) }

在我看来，这种方法是比较容易混淆的数组迭代方法之一。 传递给 .reduce() 的 reducer 回调函数的语法如下，
reduce(function(accumulator, element){...} initialValue)

 const totalArray = array . reduce ( function ( accum , element ){ return element + accum }, 0 )

上面的函数是一个简单函数的示例，它将遍历数组并返回数组中所有元素的总和。 该函数遍历数组，将当前数字作为参数传递，将该数字添加到累加器，并返回两者的总和，以便它可用于数组中的下一个数字。

它是所有方法中“表达能力最低的”，并且没有内置的返回值。 它可以代替上面列出的一些其他方法，但是建议在使用 .forEach() 之前尽可能使用其他方法之一。 下面是一个使用 .forEach() 将数组的所有值打印到控制台的函数示例。

 const array = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] array . forEach ( element => console . log ( element )) //output: 1 //output: 2 //output: 3

参考资料和有用的链接

如果没有值满足测试函数，则返回 undefined。

在数组的所有元素上运行 reducer 的最终结果是一个单一的值。

The forEach() method executes a provided function once for each array element. forEach() 方法为每个数组元素执行一次提供的函数。



原文:

Array iteration methods are by far the hardest concept I have had to grasp during my time at Flatiron School. The fact that there are quite a few of them also makes learning them not an easy task. However, with time you can understand them, and utilize their functionality in your own projects! After some time using them, I feel confident to give a walk through of the basic tasks that each iteration method can be used for!

.find() and .filter()

.find() and .filter() are very similar functions that can be called on an array. They both take a callback function as their arguments. The main difference between the two is that .find() only returns the first element that returns truthy to the callback function, while .filter() returns all elements that return truthy to the callback function.

const arr = [1, 2, 7, 45, 63] const found = arr.find(element => element > 5)
console.log(found)
//output: 7

cosnt arr = [1, 2, 7, 45, 63] const found = arr.filter(element => element > 5)
console.log(found)
//output: [7, 45, 63]

This first code example shows how a .find() would work on a simple array of just numbers. While 45 and 63 are also greater than 5, the .find() method will only return the first truthy value, which is 7. .filter() on the other hand will return 7, 45, and 63 since they are all truthy values in this scenario. It is important to note that .filter() returns the values in a new array.

.map()

.map() is called on an array and takes a callback function as the argument. It will call the callback function once on each item that is in the array, and return a new array of the mutated values.

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] const newArr = arr.map(element => element * 5)
console.log(newArr)
//output: [5, 10, 15, 20, 25]

.map() can also be used as a array copying method if needed.

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] const newArr = arr.map(element => element)
console.log(newArr)
//output: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

Both of these examples show the basic functionality of the .map() method, but .map() is very versatile and can be used in many different situations. Here is a snippet of code that I wrote to capitalize the first letter in each word of an array of strings! You will see that you can even chain .map() by calling it inside of another .map()

const titleCased = () => { return tutorials.map(function(string){ let word = string.split(' ') let capWords = word.map(function(word){ return word.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + word.substring(1) }) return capWords.join(' ') })
}

.reduce()

.reduce(), like all the other array iteration methods, takes a callback function as the argument. This method, in my opinion, is one of the more confusing array iteration methods. MDN Web Docs does a great job in explaining how it works.

"It runs a "reducer" callback function over all elements in the array, and accumulates them into a single value. Every time, the return value of callback function is passed into the function again on next invocation as accumulator. The final value of accumulator becomes the return value of reduce()." - MDN Web Docs

The syntax for a reducer callback function passed into .reduce() is as follows,
reduce(function(accumulator, element){...} initialValue)

const totalArray = array.reduce(function(accum, element){ return element + accum
}, 0)

The function above is an example of a simple function that will loop over an array and return the total of all the elements in the array. The function loops over the array, passes the current number as an argument, adds that number to the accumulator, and returns the total of the two so that it can be used for the next number in the array.

.forEach()

.forEach() is unique compared to the other iteration methods. It is the "least expressive" out of them all, and it does not have a built in return value. .forEach() does not have a specific use, but it can be useful when debugging or mutating an array. It can be used in place of some of the other methods listed above, however it is recommend to use one of the other methods if possible before using .forEach(). An example of a function using .forEach() to print all the values of an array to the console is below.

const array = [1, 2, 3] array.forEach(element => console.log(element))
//output: 1
//output: 2
//output: 3

References and Useful Links

The find() method returns the first element in the provided array that satisfies the provided testing function. If no values satisfy the testing function, undefined is returned.

The filter() method creates a shallow copy of a portion of a given array, filtered down to just the elements from the given array that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

The map() method creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

The reduce() method executes a user-supplied "reducer" callback function on each element of the array, in order, passing in the return value from the calculation on the preceding element. The final result of running the reducer across all elements of the array is a single value.

The forEach() method executes a provided function once for each array element.
